page: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized Gen5 instance designed for workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor operating at 3.1 GHz, delivering 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical core with hyper-threading. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography.

The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU hardware. In benchmark evaluations, the r5b.large demonstrates average single-core performance, scoring 1138.0 in Geekbench single-core tests. However, its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into lower performance tiers, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 1356.0 and an uncached large-block read memory bandwidth of 12.97 GB/sec.

With its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM configuration, the r5b.large offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not demand high parallel processing power. It is suited for small-scale database deployments, in-memory caching layers such as Redis—where it achieves 1.67 million SET operations per second—and lightweight enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized Gen5 instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance includes a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance alongside lower-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads with low compute requirements, making it suitable for small database deployments, caching layers, and memory-bound applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized Gen5 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, designed for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized Gen5 instance with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.1 GHz processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized dual-vCPU instance featuring a high RAM-to-core ratio for memory-bound workloads without local storage requirements.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz with 2 dedicated vCPUs
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
Built on the Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive tasks
